task.en: On the Desktop there is an image file named `banner.png` (it is wider than it is tall, 800 by 400 pixels). Open it in the Preview app, rotate the image a quarter-turn (90 degrees, using Preview's Tools menu — either direction is fine), and then save the rotated image to the Desktop as a PNG file named `banner-rotated.png`. After rotating, the saved image must be taller than it is wide (400 wide by 800 tall).

pre_command: DESK="$HOME/Desktop"; mkdir -p "$DESK"; rm -f "$DESK/banner-rotated.png"; SRC=""; for C in '/System/Library/CoreServices/DefaultDesktop.heic' '/System/Library/Desktop Pictures/.thumbnails/Big Sur Coastline.madesktop' '/System/Library/CoreServices/Finder.app/Contents/Resources/Finder.icns' '/System/Applications/Preview.app/Contents/Resources/AppIcon.icns' '/System/Library/CoreServices/CoreTypes.bundle/Contents/Resources/GenericApplicationIcon.icns'; do [ -e "$C" ] && { SRC="$C"; break; }; done; [ -n "$SRC" ] && sips -s format png -z 400 800 "$SRC" --out "$DESK/banner.png" >/dev/null 2>&1; W=$(sips -g pixelWidth "$DESK/banner.png" 2>/dev/null | awk '/pixelWidth/{print $2}'); [ "$W" = "800" ] || sips -s format png --resampleHeightWidth 400 800 "$DESK/banner.png" --out "$DESK/banner.png" >/dev/null 2>&1
grading_command: OUT="$HOME/Desktop/banner-rotated.png"; [ -f "$OUT" ] || { echo False; exit 0; }; F=$(sips -g format "$OUT" 2>/dev/null | awk '/format:/{print $2}'); [ "$F" = "png" ] && echo True || echo False

OUT="$HOME/Desktop/banner-rotated.png"; [ -f "$OUT" ] || { echo False; exit 0; }; W=$(sips -g pixelWidth "$OUT" 2>/dev/null | awk '/pixelWidth/{print $2}'); H=$(sips -g pixelHeight "$OUT" 2>/dev/null | awk '/pixelHeight/{print $2}'); [ "$W" = "400" ] && [ "$H" = "800" ] && echo True || echo False
